Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng, for and with petitioner Andrea Leung.

The Court inquired if the petition should be granted in the absence of resignations of the current trustees. The Court further indicated his concern that under the setlement agreement the trustee would be precluded from administering the business which is a trust asset. Ms. Ng responded that she had obtained the resignations and was in the process of getting them filed. She added that the business was in the process of being purchased by a family member (Ming Luk's daughter Jennifer), so the trustee would not need to administer the business. She said that this is a family business where Mrs. Luk spends her days, but that Mrs. Luk has not run the business for many years as it is being run by other members of the family. The Court stated that he would grant the petition, but would strike the limitation on liability provision. He requested that Ms. Ng provide an amended order which provided for an appropriate bond. The Court granted the petition (subject to receipt and review of the order).

Off Calendar May-31-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Andrea Leung. Court Appointed Counsel Christina Chen for Shao Lan Luk. William Taylor for Chiu Luk & Shao Luk. Hannah Salassi for Jennifer Lu. Terry Luk, Peter Luk and Chiu Luk are present in court.

The Court stated that there are no oppositions filed, that in regards to a couple of the properties 80% ownership is identified but 20% is not, that one of the signatories is Colleen Yu, that it suggests it is being signed for by a parent, that it is not clear who she is or if the court should be concerned if a Guardian ad Litem is needed. Mr. Taylor stated that Colleen Yu is named in one cause of action in the civil complaint, that the suit has settled, that she does not have and is not asserting a legal claim and that she is a family member of Ms. Lu. Ms. Salassi stated that she represents Colleen in the civil suit, that Mr. Taylor is correct, that none are direct beneficiaries of the trust or assert claims, confirms that she is a minor, that regarding the current interest the settlement agreement resolves the interest in the properties and that it does not matter what the current ownership interests are. The Court asked who owns the other 20% interest. Ms. Salassi stated that Chiu Luk, Jennifer Lu and Shao Luk own it. Mr. Taylor stated that all of the people on the title or have asserted claims have signed the settlement. Ms. Ng stated that they filed a supplement addressing the email from the examiner and filed waivers of notice yesterday. The Court asked if the waivers were from the Yu group and Ms. Salassi confirmed that they were, and asked if there was any effect of this petition on the remaining issues on 6/22/16. Ms. Ng stated that the only remaining petition would be her clients petition for appointment as general trustee. Ms. Salassi requested that it be continued until after the sales of property. Ms. Chen stated that if there is a sale of real property there should be a trustee. The Court granted the petition for order approving settlement agreement (subject to review of the order) and stated that all other petitions set for 6/22/16 are off calendar with the exception of the petition for the appointment of successor trustee by Andrea Leung.

Jennifer Run Ping Lus Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust, To Appoint Professional Trustee, For Damages For Breach Of Trust, For Payment Of Attorneys Fees, And For Removal Of Susan Yee As Attorney For Shao Lan Luk
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Continued To Mar-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Andrea Leung. William Taylor for Shao Lan Luk & Chiu Luk. Hannah Salassi for (cross petitioner) Jennifer Lu. Court appointed counsel Christina Chen for (conservatee) Shao Lan Luk.

The Court asked if the parties had figured out a resolution. Mr. Taylor reported that it appears they ar on their way to doing that, that they have had two mediation sessions, that they have had extensive communication, that an additional mediation date has been sought and provided, that the accounting is done, that the parties have a framework and that the details should not be difficult. Ms. Salassi confirmed that everyone is hopeful, that they have a good framework, that she, Ms. Leung and Ms. Chen went through the records at the printing company and did a rough inventory and that they have an accountant lined up. Ms. Chen stated that if settlement eludes them the accounting should be done and paid for by the parties not the trust and would like an order from the court. Mr. Taylor stated that in the related civil proceeding they have an April 11, 2016 trial date, that they have sufficient outside constraints to get it done now and that if an accounting needs to be done the start date should be deferred. Ms. Salassi stated that the civil suit deadlines are an impetus to settle and that they agree that the accounting should be deferred. Ms. Chen stated that there is an issue as to who represents the conservatee in the litigation, that Ms. Chen's authority was extended to this case but not the civil litigation, that Suzanne Yee's office is in conflict and that part of the petition today is for the removal of that law office. The Court confirmed that they are asking the court to not wait for a settlement but to rule now. Ms. Chen stated that they have a framework but need to remove the conservatee from the litigation. Mr. Taylor stated that it is opposed, that he did not understand that it was on calendar today, that there is history as to why they filed the conservatorship, that it is the result of defendant Ms. Lu, that there are concerns regarding the conservator being willing to give up the elder abuse litigation and that Ms. Chen's role is only to settle the trust. The Court stated that the issue to not pursue the elder abuse is not before the court, that the conservator can pursue it and that it is not constructive to fight over other relief requested when settlement is right around the corner. Ms. Salassi requested a short continuance stating that they will know then if they have or have not settled. Mr. Taylor stated that they should be able to work out a stipulation regarding the payment for the accounting. The petition to remove trustee for breach of trust, etc., the petition to remove trustee, surcharge of trustee and for an accounting, and the petition for appointment of successor trustee and for trustee bond were continued to March 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of settlement and that if it is not resolved status on what is happening.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
